briefs.title: 190 residents sign petition opposing rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council received a petition signed by 190 residents opposing rezoning application REZ #25-13 at 5058 Imperial Street. The petition was noted for information, meaning Council has seen it, but no decision on the rezoning has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor Burnaby Council meetings for a rezoning decision.

briefs.title: Burnaby updates how bylaw notices are enforced — rules now in effect
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has formally passed Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, updating the rules around how bylaw notices (official written warnings or tickets for breaking a city rule) are enforced. This change adjusts the existing 2009 Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw, which sets out the process for issuing and contesting those notices.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business has any open bylaw notices in Burnaby — new enforcement rules may affect your options.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Burnaby raises municipal fees and charges — all businesses paying city fees affected
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, updating its consolidated fees and charges bylaw. This means the official schedule of fees the City of Burnaby charges for permits, licences, inspections, and other municipal services has been formally changed.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to see if any permits or licences you pay for have changed in cost.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby updates rules for parking meters and EV charging meters
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed an amendment to its Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw, updating the rules that govern how parking meters and EV (electric vehicle) charging stations are regulated in the city. Businesses that operate, manage, or rely on parking or EV charging in Burnaby should be aware that new rules are now in effect.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates or manages parking or EV charging meters in Burnaby and review the updated bylaw.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Burnaby Rezones 5058 Imperial Street — What Nearby Property Owners Should Know
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council gave first and second reading (early approval steps in a multi-stage process) to a zoning bylaw amendment for 5058 Imperial Street. This means the city is moving forward with changing what can legally be built or operated at that address, though four council members voted against it.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor future council readings and public hearings for this rezoning.

briefs.title: Burnaby Rezones 2209 & 2225 Springer Ave — Land Use Change Moves Forward
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to the rules about how land can be used) for two properties at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. This moves the rezoning application forward, but it is not yet final — further readings and approval are still required.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to develop property near Springer Ave, monitor this rezoning for its final approval.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council advances rezoning of 6800 Lougheed Highway through first and second reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has given first and second reading to a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to the rules about how a specific piece of land can be used) for the property at 6800 Lougheed Highway. This moves the rezoning application forward in the approval process, with further readings and a public hearing still required before any change is finalized.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing notice for 6800 Lougheed Highway if your business may be affected by this rezoning.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project now underway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has received an overview of the project initiation for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan. An OCP is a long-term planning document that guides how land in the city can be used and developed — this action plan sets out the steps to put those goals into practice.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: City investment program results for 2025 reviewed; 2026 forecast presented to Council
briefs.summary: City Council received a report from its Financial Management Committee summarizing how the city's investment program performed in 2025 and what returns are expected in 2026. This is an informational update only — no new rules, fees, or requirements for businesses were introduced.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council endorses Poverty Reduction Strategy; implementation plan and provincial funding push underway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially approved a Poverty Reduction Strategy and directed staff to build an implementation plan alongside community partners. The Mayor will also write to the provincial government to request funding to support poverty reduction efforts in the city.

briefs.title: Five-storey self-storage facility approved for 2757 Norland Avenue — rezoning bylaw in progress
briefs.summary: City council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (an official rule change that allows a new type of building or use on a specific property) for 2757 Norland Avenue, allowing a five-storey self-storage facility to be built there. No public hearing is required because the proposal already fits the city's Official Community Plan (the long-term land-use blueprint for the area).
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 2757 Norland Avenue, monitor the rezoning bylaw adoption for impacts on local traffic or parking.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 12-storey, 290-unit mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway moves closer to approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (a formal rule change that allows a different type of building on a property) for 6535 Kingsway. If approved, the site will allow a 12-storey building with 290 homes and ground-floor commercial space.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meetings for the rezoning bylaw vote if this site affects your business or property plans.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Grand Promenade rezoning adds 362 strata units and hotel use, removes office space
briefs.summary: City council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (an official rule change that controls how land can be used) for the Grand Promenade development. The approved plan now swaps out office space for a hotel and restaurant, adds 362 new strata (privately owned) residential units, and moves the required affordable housing to a nearby site called Carrigan.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the rezoning bylaw process if your business is near Grand Promenade or depends on that development.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: Carrigan Court mixed-use development rezoning application accepted by council
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has authorized staff to accept a rezoning application (a formal request to change how land can be used) for 3856–3870 Carrigan Court. The proposed development would bring high-density housing — both strata (individually owned units) and rental — alongside commercial space to that site.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Carrigan Court, monitor rezoning progress for potential impacts on your location.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Municipal asphalt paving contract worth $1.155M renewed with Blackrete Paving
briefs.summary: A local council has renewed its asphalt paving services contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for an estimated $1,155,000 including taxes. This is a government procurement decision (a contract awarded by a public body to a private company) and does not introduce new rules or obligations for most small businesses.

briefs.title: 19 contractors awarded hired equipment services contracts paid by actual quantities delivered
briefs.summary: A local council has approved contracts with 19 separate contractors to provide hired equipment services. Payments to these contractors will be based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, rather than a fixed price agreed in advance.

briefs.title: Modular Four-Plex Pilot Contract Sent Back for Efficiency Review Before Award
briefs.summary: City council has paused the contract award for a modular housing four-plex pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue, sending it back to staff for further review. The goal is to find ways to manage the project more efficiently before any contract is signed.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council has awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology. This is a public procurement (government purchasing) decision and does not directly change rules or regulations for private businesses.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: A BC municipal council has approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) for 2026. UBCM is the association that represents BC's local governments and advocates on their behalf to provincial and federal governments.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority approves 2026–2030 Strategic Plan, shaping city housing development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) Corporation's five-year strategic plan for 2026–2030. This plan will guide how the BHA develops and manages affordable housing in Burnaby over the next five years, which may affect local construction, development, and property management activity.
